message.content:
Adversaries may perform Internet Connection Discovery to check for Internet connectivity on compromised systems, aiding in automated discovery and communication with Command and Control (C2) servers. This technique involves using tools like Ping, tracert, and HTTP GET requests to determine if a system can connect to external networks. The results help adversaries identify routes, redirectors, and proxy servers, ensuring reliable communication with their C2 infrastructure. Notable groups such as APT29, Gamaredon Group, and Turla have employed various methods, including GoldFinder, BITSAdmin, and custom scripts, to test connectivity. The impact of this technique includes enhanced adversary situational awareness and improved C2 communication reliability.

Mitigation strategies include monitoring network traffic for unusual patterns, implementing strict outbound traffic controls, and using endpoint detection and response (EDR) solutions to detect and block suspicious network discovery activities. Regularly updating and patching systems can also reduce the risk of exploitation.
